title: Streaming Carbon Footprint Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Calculate CO₂ emissions from your streaming hours (Netflix, YouTube). See your monthly carbon footprint. Fast, free.
seoKeywords: how much carbon does streaming use
intro: Your carbon footprint measures the total environmental impact of your activities. Understanding your CO₂ emissions helps you reduce them through concrete actions—transportation, energy use, consumption, and food choices. Calculate the CO₂ generated by your monthly streaming hours (Netflix, YouTube) and see how your viewing habits affect the planet. This calculator uses 2026 data and is updated regularly to keep you informed.
keyTakeaway: **Formula: ~36 g CO₂ per hour of HD video (global average)**. The actual emissions depend on video quality (SD, HD, 4K) and your connection type (WiFi uses 60% less than 4G/5G). Use this calculator to estimate your monthly streaming carbon impact.

fields.label: Hours per day
fields.help: How many hours of streaming per day.

explanation:
Environmental sustainability is a critical issue for the 21st century. Measuring your impact—carbon, water, waste—and reducing it through concrete actions is both an individual and collective responsibility.

## How the calculation works

This calculator uses the formula: **CO₂ (g/hour) = base emission rate × video quality factor**

Base rates vary by streaming service due to server efficiency and network infrastructure. The formula accounts for:
- **Video quality**: SD (10g/hr) < HD (36g/hr) < 4K (75g/hr)
- **Connection type**: WiFi uses ~60% less energy than 4G/5G
- **Device type**: Displays (especially OLED with dark mode) affect power consumption

This calculator applies the formula with your inputs and alerts you to values outside typical ranges.

## Key takeaways

Streaming has become a major part of daily life. While individual hours may seem small, collective usage drives significant energy consumption and carbon emissions. Using WiFi, choosing lower video resolution when possible, and enabling dark mode are practical ways to reduce your digital carbon footprint.

faq.q: How much CO₂ is produced per hour of streaming?
faq.a: Standard Definition (SD): ~10g/hr. High Definition (HD): ~36g/hr. 4K: ~75g/hr. Most emissions come from data center servers and network infrastructure.

faq.q: Does WiFi vs. mobile (4G/5G) make a difference?
faq.a: Yes. WiFi uses about 60% less energy than cellular networks. Always use WiFi at home when available to reduce your streaming emissions.

faq.q: Does dark mode actually reduce carbon emissions?
faq.a: On OLED displays, yes. Dark mode can reduce power consumption by ~30% because OLED pixels emit their own light. YouTube dark theme is an easy way to help.

faq.q: Is streaming from Netflix vs. YouTube different?
faq.a: Similar overall impact. Services with better video compression (often YouTube) tend to emit slightly less CO₂ per hour of viewing.

faq.q: Is it better to download than stream?
faq.a: If you'll watch more than twice, downloading saves energy. For one-time viewing, the impact is similar—download bandwidth isn't free.
These are estimates using internationally validated formulas. Actual emissions vary by region (grid carbon intensity), service, and device. For critical decisions, consult a professional.
Yes. All Hacé Cuentas calculators are completely free with no registration required.

faq.q: Do you store my data or track my calculations?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser. We don't send any of your data to our servers.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data updates required
